mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapidly rising unemployment signals deteriorating consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or the signal shows no predictive power in recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in consumer credit outstanding and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and household balance sheet strength, supporting corporate earnings growth and driving positive equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample testing, or forward returns are flat when both series rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which lowers input cost inflation and boosts corporate margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rising INDPRO correlates with negative equity returns due to supply-driven disinflation compressing revenue growth, or Rank IC drops below zero over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Steady quarterly growth in real GDP provides a fundamental earnings floor for corporate America, mechanically supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon as analyst revisions turn favorable.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth periods consistently yield negative or flat equity returns, indicating decoupling between macro growth and corporate profitability.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates exceeding a 3.0% threshold signal restrictive monetary policy that suppresses equity multiples and increases borrowing costs, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when rates cross above 3.0%, or the threshold signal generates more false positives than true negatives in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates a healthy wage-price equilibrium without overheating, fostering a risk-on environment that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows negative or insignificant coefficients in a regression of forward returns, or high payroll growth coincides with equity drawdowns due to inflation fears.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in housing starts reflect improving consumer confidence and construction sector momentum, which historically precede broad-based economic recovery and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Rising housing starts fail to predict positive equity returns, or the signal reverses direction during periods of speculative housing bubbles.
